review_notes:
Confirm the benchmark runner executes from the staged input root, because `read_data water_216_data.lmp` is a relative path. Confirm the runner injects `timer full` or equivalent, since the staged input scripts themselves only define thermo output. Because the staged inputs use `units electron`, the standard LAMMPS performance line will report `fs/day` rather than `ns/day`. The provided inputs do not activate `kspace_modify diff ad`, slab correction, triclinic logic, or PPPM per-atom energy/virial interpolation, so those code paths need separate validation only if you intend to optimize or rely on them. The `most.cmake` preset enables the OPENMP package, but the staged inputs do not request `/omp` styles, so with `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1` the intended hot path remains the base editable source files.
Verify `FERMILINK_GOAL_INPUT_ROOT` points at the staged files intended for authoritative runs; the YAML defaults it to `.fermilink-optimize/inputs/all`, and the runner resolves relative values against the repo root.

Verify the benchmark environment can satisfy `mpirun -np 16`, `32`, and `64`, and that the pre-command produces `build/lmp` at the repo root before runs start.

Verify the correctness force field is acceptable as a deterministic 256-atom evenly spaced sample from the final `write_dump` snapshot rather than a full 41,472-atom force vector, since that keeps runner JSON and artifact size bounded.

Verify injecting `timer full` and the final `write_dump` through a temporary wrapper input is acceptable for your harness; this leaves the staged input scripts unchanged while ensuring the runner can parse full timing-table metrics and sampled final forces.

I syntax-checked the runner with `python3 -m py_compile` and loaded the YAML with PyYAML, but I did not execute the full MPI benchmark in this turn.
